//: LESSON 198 — Generated by 'npm create vite@latest vite-demo-ts -- --template vanilla-ts'. The 'dev' script starts a local hot-reload dev server. The 'build' script runs tsc for TYPE CHECKING (no emit) and then vite build to produce the bundled, minified, hashed output in dist/. The 'preview' script serves the built dist/ folder locally so you can verify the production output before deploying. devDependencies: typescript stays here for type checking, vite is the build tool & dev server.
//lesson-199: Why TypeScript is a devDependency even though it is installed globally: (1) the build script can run on ANY machine (e.g., a CI server or a teammate's laptop) without requiring a global tsc install — 'npm install' pulls the local copy into node_modules/. (2) Each project can pin its OWN TypeScript version via the '~6.0.2' spec here. If one project is locked to an older compiler (say, TS 4.x) because upgrading would break things, and another project needs the latest TS, they can coexist without conflict. The global tsc is only relevant for ad-hoc commands outside any project.
name: vite-demo-ts
